Perhaps the text is: "Yɛlimaŋni, ka n siri yɛtti yaɛ, ni u nin ti zaŋŋi u bunseelli kam fukumsi, n-niŋi lala daabri nuu ni."

So: "Truly, I refuse to tell you that he will take his every good thing with power; I do such work with hand."

This still doesn't make sense.

Another idea: "bunseelli" might be "creation" or "thing made," and "fukumsi" might be "control" or "command."

"n-niŋi" might be "I am doing" or "I will do."

"lala daabri" – "such work."

"nuu ni" – "by hand" or "manually."

So, perhaps: "Truly, I refuse to tell you that he will take control of every good thing of his; I do such work by hand."

But why would he take control of his own good things? That doesn't make sense.
